title: Better Days Display
description: Track up to 10,000 survival days with a visual day counter, unique milestone rewards, and surprises on special days in your Minecraft world.
body:
Better Days Display

A polished and multiplayer-friendly day counter datapack for Minecraft 1.21.x, with milestone celebrations, rewards, and dedicated version packages for full compatibility.

Track your survival journey day after day, celebrate key milestones, and add a little extra life to your world without cluttering your gameplay.

Settings command: /function betterdays:settings/open

🌟 Features

  • Visual Day Counter: Displays the current survived day in the action bar in a clean vanilla-friendly style.
  • Milestone Celebrations: Unique milestone moments for days like 1, 7, 13, 25, 42, 50, 75, every hundred, legendary numbers like 404, 666, 777, 888, 999, and every thousand up to 10,000.
  • Special Effects & Rewards: Some milestones include fun effects, sounds, or small thematic rewards to celebrate progress.
  • Roleplay Flavor: Milestones use custom titles and messages instead of generic “milestone reached” text.
  • Per-Player Progress: Each player tracks their own survived days independently in multiplayer.
  • Robust Day Tracking: The datapack tracks survived days per player using a safer internal system designed for regular long-term worlds.
  • One-Time Milestones: Milestone events trigger once per player, avoiding spam and duplicate rewards.
  • Lightweight: No mods required, no setup commands for normal use, just install and play.
  • Version-Specific Releases: Separate packaged versions are prepared for the different Minecraft 1.21.x datapack formats for the best compatibility possible.

📦 Minecraft Version Support

This project supports the Minecraft 1.21.x family through separate ready-to-use datapack packages.

  • 1.21 - 1.21.1
  • 1.21.2 - 1.21.4
  • 1.21.5
  • 1.21.6 - 1.21.8
  • 1.21.9 - 1.21.10
  • 1.21.11

Important: download the zip that matches your exact Minecraft version.

📦 Installation

  1. Download the zip for your exact Minecraft version.
  2. Place it in your world’s datapacks folder.
  3. Run /reload in-game if needed.
  4. Survive and watch your day counter grow.

This is a datapack, so it works in vanilla, modded Minecraft, singleplayer, and servers as long as the correct version package is used.

You can also use a global datapack loader if you want it available in every world.

To open the settings menu in-game: /function betterdays:settings/open

🧩 What’s New in v1.1

  • First polished public release of Better Days Display.
  • Cleaner internal structure and more consistent datapack logic.
  • Improved survived-day tracking and safer initialization flow.
  • Dedicated release packages for the Minecraft 1.21.x version line.
  • Better overall reliability for long-term worlds and multiplayer use.
